exemplar.title: What is the effect of varying ethanol concentration (0%, 5%, 10%, 15%, 20%, 25%) on the inhibition of Î±-amylase enzyme activity on starch in a solution, as measured by the time taken for the iodine-starch complex to disappear caused by an iodine-starch complex at room temperature (25Â°C)?

detailedExemplarData.title: What is the effect of varying ethanol concentration (0%, 5%, 10%, 15%, 20%, 25%) on the inhibition of Î±-amylase enzyme activity on starch in a solution, as measured by the time taken for the iodine-starch complex to disappear caused by an iodine-starch complex at room temperature (25Â°C)?

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Methodological considerations are not just listed but fully explained with clear links to data quality.
The step-by-step procedure is detailed enough (volumes, times, apparatus) to allow straightforward replication.
The research question is situated in a specific biochemical and medical context, demonstrating strong background understanding.
weaknesses: The justification for the chosen ethanol concentration range lacks a direct citation to enzymology or dietary studies.
The controlled-variables table uses ambiguous âdropsâ without specifying volume per drop.
Terminology around inhibition type is inconsistent (mixed vs non-competitive).
Evaporation control is acknowledged later but not integrated into the controlled-variables table.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each of the three strands in Research Design was judged to meet the descriptor for band 5 in the 5â6 range: context is specific, methodological considerations are explained, and methodology description permits replication. Minor ambiguities (drop volume, citation for concentration range, inconsistent terminology, and omitted evaporation control) prevent a top-band 6, so a score of 5 accurately reflects the high but not flawless performance.

strengths: Data recording and processing are both clear and precise: well-organised tables, correctly stated uncertainties, labelled graphs with error bars.
Data processing is thorough and appropriate: means, standard deviations, reciprocal conversions, ANOVA and Tukey HSD are correctly applied and documented.
weaknesses: Uncertainties are not propagated into the calculated reaction rates.
Inconsistency between stated number of trials (five) and raw data (seven) undermines statistical integrity.
Several formatting and reporting issues: missing units on rate column, misreported p-value inequality, and inconsistent notation in Tukey results.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The criterion meets the topâband descriptor for Communication of data and Data processing quality with clear, precise presentation and accurate processing, despite minor errors. Consideration of uncertainties is meaningful but incomplete, placing that strand in band 4. Overall, the strong data handling and correct use of statistical tests justify band 5 (upper half of 3â4 for uncertainty and 5â6 elsewhere), so a holistic score of 5 is warranted.

strengths: The conclusion directly answers the research question and is consistent with the data analysis, citing significance tests and observed trends.
Relevant scientific context is used to justify findings, comparing mixed-inhibition results to biochemical and glycaemic control literature.
weaknesses: Terminology alternates between âmixedâ and ânon-competitiveâ inhibition, creating conceptual confusion.
Quantitative linkage to specific rate values or slopes is limited, weakening the evidential justification.
Interpretations (e.g., enzyme denaturation causing plateau) are not supported by additional assays.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands of the Conclusion criterion demonstrate high-band performance: relevance and consistency with analysis, and contextualization within accepted scientific literature. Minor conceptual inconsistencies and limited quantitative linkage prevent a perfect 6. The overall strength of the conclusion warrants a score of 5.

strengths: Methodological weaknesses are specifically identified and their likely impacts on data quality are clearly explained.
Proposed improvements are realistic, directly tied to the identified limitations, and include clear rationale for enhancing accuracy and applicability.
weaknesses: No quantitative estimate of how much factors like pH shifts or evaporation could affect reaction rates.
Some improvement suggestions (e.g., spectrophotometer use) lack detailed methodological parameters (wavelength, calibration curve).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation demonstrates band-5 performance in both strands: specific limitations are explained with impact, and improvements are explained in relation to those weaknesses. The absence of quantitative impact assessment and detailed procedural specifics stops short of band-6. A holistic score of 5 accurately reflects the criterionâs high quality with modest omissions.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The conclusion directly answers the research question, cites mean values and significance tests, and discusses plateauing inhibition, matching the presented data.  
â¢ Logical consistency is maintained with no contradictory statements; however, quantitative linkage to rate values (e.g., explicit slope) is limited.  
â¢ This justifies a high, but not perfect, score of 5 in the top band.

Raw data tables list every trial with instrument uncertainties, processed tables show means, SD, and reciprocal rates, and graphs include error bars and labelled axes.  
â¢ The presentation is **both clear (well-organised tables/figures) and precise (uncertainties stated to correct significant figures)**.  
â¢ Occasional formatting inconsistencies (empty columns, misplaced units) prevent the very top mark; thus a 5 in the top band is appropriate.

Data processing encompasses means, standard deviations, reciprocal rate conversion, one-way ANOVA, and Tukey HSD. Calculations are shown with sample workings and values are internally consistent with raw data.  
â¢ The statistical tests appropriately address the research question and support conclusions.  
â¢ A very small arithmetic inconsistency in the ANOVA explanation (p-value wording) prevents a perfect 6; hence a solid 5 is assigned.

Specific weaknesses are **explained with their likely impact**: variability at 20 % ethanol attributed to set-up inconsistencies, subjectivity of colour change, untested higher concentrations, uncontrolled evaporation, etc.  
â¢ The student comments on how each could alter enzyme activity or data spread, satisfying the âexplain impactâ requirement.  
â¢ Explanations are clear but not exhaustive (e.g., no quantitative error estimate), so a 5 in the top band fits.

The student compares findings to accepted concepts such as mixed inhibition, Michaelis-Menten kinetics, and real-world glycaemic control, citing relevant literature.  
â¢ The discussion is more than superficial, weaving experimental evidence with published studies.  
â¢ Yet, numerical comparison to literature values of Km/Vmax is absent, so a 5 (not 6) in the highest band is most appropriate.

The student places the research question inside a **specific biochemical and medical context**, explaining the mixed-inhibition nature of ethanol, linking chosen concentrations to real alcoholic beverages, and outlining metabolic consequences (post-prandial glucose control).  
â¢ This level of detail clearly locates the investigation within a relevant scientific framework rather than only mentioning a broad topic.  
â¢ Minor gaps remain (e.g., no direct citation for concentration range selection within enzymology literature), so the strand is strong but not flawless; a score at the lower end of the 5-6 band is therefore appropriate.

Uncertainties for timing, volume and temperature instruments are stated and the graph includes SD error bars, demonstrating a **meaningful but imperfect consideration**.  
â¢ There is no propagation of instrumental uncertainty into the calculated rate, and error bars reflect SD rather than combined uncertainty.  
â¢ These omissions place the work firmly in the 3-4 band; the inclusion of some relevant uncertainty treatment merits the upper value of 4.

Improvements (use of spectrophotometer, extended ethanol range, kinetic analysis, testing reversibility) are **directly linked to stated weaknesses** and their benefits are articulated, demonstrating a clear rationale.  
â¢ The student explains how each suggestion would reduce subjectivity, increase accuracy, or broaden applicability, meeting the âexplainedâ descriptor.  
â¢ Some proposals remain qualitative rather than detailing exact methodological changes, so a score of 5 is awarded within the highest band.

Methodological issues (control of temperature, pH, substrate/enzyme volumes, mixing, timing, safety) are **explained rather than merely listed**. The student justifies why each factor could influence amylase activity and how it is managed, showing a clear link between controls and data quality.  
â¢ The considerations are relevant and sufficient for answering the research question.  
â¢ Some finer points (e.g., evaporation of ethanol, accuracy of "drops") are only acknowledged later, so the explanation is not exhaustive; a mid-high score of 5 within the top band is warranted.

The step-by-step procedure includes exact volumes, concentrations, incubation times, stirring speed, number of trials, and cleaning steps, allowing **straightforward replication**.  
â¢ Photographs and tables of solution preparation reinforce clarity.  
â¢ Minor ambiguities remain (undefined volume of a âdropâ, omission of pipette size for amylase), preventing a perfect 6; nevertheless the description fully permits reproduction, justifying a score of 5 in the highest band.

annotations.comment: The research question is well situated in a medical and biochemical framework but lacks a direct citation justifying the chosen ethanol concentration range. Consider referencing enzymology literature or dietary ethanol studies to support why 0â25 % is appropriate for investigating mixed inhibition.

The introduction provides a concise biochemical explanation of mixed inhibition, linking ethanolâs effects on Km and Vmax to conformational changes. This depth of background demonstrates strong contextualization of the research question.

The hypothesis describes ethanol as a mixed inhibitor, yet other sections refer to non-competitive inhibition. Clarify and consistently define the inhibition type across the document to avoid conceptual confusion.

The dependent variable section mentions spectrophotometric measurement at 620 nm, but the procedure relies on visual timing. Ensure the methodology aligns with the stated objective measurement to minimize subjectivity.
In the controlledâvariables table, â5 drops per trialâ is ambiguous. Specify the volume per drop (for example, in mL) or use calibrated pipetting to improve reproducibility.
Ethanol evaporation is acknowledged later but omitted from the controlled variables table. Include evaporation rate or cover test tubes to prevent concentration changes during incubations.
The materials and apparatus table is comprehensive and clearly lists uncertainties for each measurement device, enhancing the studyâs reproducibility.
Step 1 in solution preparation gives exact volumes for each ethanol concentration (e.g., 1 cmÂ³ + 19 cmÂ³), which allows for clear replication of stock solutions.

The qualitative observations are insightful but are not referenced in the quantitative analysis. Consider correlating observed colorâfade patterns with measured rates to strengthen data interpretation.
The raw data table for trials lists seven measurements per concentration, yet the method states five trials. Resolve this inconsistency to ensure accurate statistical analysis.
Uncertainties are listed for timing and volume, but their propagation into the calculated reaction rates is missing. Include uncertainty propagation in 1/time to reflect combined error.
The rate column lacks explicit units (e.g., sâ»Â¹). Always include units to ensure clarity and avoid ambiguity in data presentation.
The sample calculation for the rate uses 0.022 s without showing the reciprocal calculation to sufficient significant figures. Show intermediate steps and rounding justifications.
The ANOVA results narrative states âp-value > 0.05â when in fact 4.98Ã10â»Â¹â¹Â³ < 0.05. Correct this inequality to accurately reflect statistical significance.
In the post hoc Tukey table, p-adj values are displayed as 0 but lack inequality formatting (e.g., p < 0.001). Use consistent notation to avoid misinterpretation.

While the conclusion summarizes key findings, it alternates between âmixedâ and ânon-competitiveâ inhibition. Align this terminology with the hypothesis and background for conceptual coherence.
The conclusion discusses inhibitory effects quantitatively but would benefit from explicit linkage to measured rate values (e.g., slope or percentage change) for stronger justification.

In the conclusion, plateaus in inhibition are ascribed to enzyme denaturation and starch solubility, yet no denaturation assay was performed. Suggest qualifying this interpretation or including spectroscopic checks.

The evaluation notes pH shifts and evaporation as uncontrolled factors but does not explain their potential impact magnitude. Assess how pH deviations of Â±0.1 or ethanol loss might alter reaction rates.
The recommendation to use spectrophotometric quantitation is valid but should specify wavelength, calibration curves, and sample handling to demonstrate how precision will improve.

The sample calculation for preparing ethanol solutions uses total volume = 100 mL, but the experiment uses 20 mL. Check this formula context to ensure consistency between calculation and actual protocol.
The ANOVA table includes F crit but the text does not compare F (723 482.50) against F crit (2.323). Explicitly state F > F crit to reinforce rejection of the null hypothesis.
Interpretation of Tukey HSD results states that all comparisons are significant but does not discuss effect sizes or confidence intervals. Include these to add depth to statistical conclusions.
It would strengthen the evaluation to include MichaelisâMenten kinetics, calculating Km and Vmax for each ethanol concentration to quantify inhibition mechanisms.

strands.content: The communication of the recording and processing of the data is either clear or precise.

strands.content: The recording and processing of data shows evidence of a consideration of uncertainties but with some significant omissions or inaccuracies.

strands.name: Data processing quality
strands.content: The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with some significant omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.

strands.name: Communication of data recording and processing
strands.content: The communication of the recording and processing of the data is both clear and precise.

strands.name: Consideration of uncertainties
strands.content: The recording and processing of data shows evidence of an appropriate consideration of uncertainties.

strands.name: Data processing quality
strands.content: The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out appropriately and accurately.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency

strands.name: Scientific context comparison

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: A conclusion is described that makes some relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: A conclusion is justified that is relevant to the research question and fully consistent with the analysis presented.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: A conclusion is justified through relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.

strands.name: Improvements

strands.name: Methodological weaknesses
strands.content: The report describes specific methodological weaknesses or limitations.

strands.name: Improvements
strands.content: Realistic improvements to the investigation that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are described.

strands.name: Methodological weaknesses

strands.name: Improvements
strands.content: Realistic improvements to the investigation, that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are explained.
